Alias.title: Alias
Alias.description:
An alias — a name for an EXISTING object (§6.3b). Unresolved *references* are `links`
rows carrying `unresolved_text`, not aliases (one mechanism, not two).

id.description: Identity of an alias (`aliases` row, §6.3b) — a name for an object.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

kind.description: How an alias came to name an object (§6.3b).

scope.description: The scope an alias is valid in (§6.3b); paired with `scope_ref` (§6.1a consistency).

scope_ref.description:
The scoping entity (source, trail, note…), polymorphic — core-validated against
`scope` (§6.1a). `None` for global scope.

AliasKind.title: AliasKind
AliasKind.description: How an alias came to name an object (§6.3b).

AliasScope.title: AliasScope
AliasScope.description: The scope an alias is valid in (§6.3b); paired with `scope_ref` (§6.1a consistency).

AssetChecksum.title: AssetChecksum
AssetChecksum.description: A content-hashed asset reference (§10). The hash is glue-computed; the core only carries it.

CanonicalObject.title: CanonicalObject
CanonicalObject.description:
A canonical object — the doc's §6 `objects` row shape, verbatim (all eleven fields).
Content (`content_units`/MathContent) is a SEPARATE aggregate arriving in slice 1;
an object with zero units is valid, which is what makes that addition purely
structural. Named `CanonicalObject` (not `Object`) so the generated TS type never
shadows JavaScript's global `Object`.

id.description: Client-minted UUIDv7 (arch doc §4/§6.3).

type.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

title.description: Tri-state (§6.3): `None` = unset, `Some("")` = explicitly empty — never collapsed.

raw_source.description: The rough input, preserved VERBATIM (§2.2). No normalization function exists.

status.description:
Object lifecycle (arch doc §5.2/§6) — the full, doc-stable set lands now even though
the skeleton's create path can only PRODUCE `Draft`; other values become producible
via future status-transition operations.

schema_version.description: Application-level model version (§6.3); migrated by the core's total functions.

revision.description: Optimistic-concurrency token (§6.4): increments on every persisted write.

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

CharSpan.title: CharSpan
CharSpan.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

oneOf.description: A span within the unit's prose text.
A span within an expression's surface — stable across inline/display toggles (§6.3a).

expression_id.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

ContentLocator.title: ContentLocator
ContentLocator.description:
Where in a unit's content an edge sits (§6.1d). Paired with the `source_unit_id`
column (the unit is context-supplied). The SQL `in_expression` column is DERIVED from
`kind = expression_span` — never written independently.

oneOf.description:
A domain validation failure. Errors are VALUES crossing the FFI (result envelopes),
never exceptions (arch doc §17 boundary discipline).
Client-minted ids must be UUIDv7 (arch doc §4/§6.3 — sortable, client-mintable).
First §6.1a origin-field invariant: `created_by` is required when origin = user.

origin.description:
Provenance origin (arch doc §6.1). Stable, complete set; only `User` and `System`
are producible until the AI/import provenance columns land with their slices.

oneOf.description: AI/import provenance is structurally impossible until its columns land (§6.1).

origin.description:
Provenance origin (arch doc §6.1). Stable, complete set; only `User` and `System`
are producible until the AI/import provenance columns land with their slices.

oneOf.description:
A stored value claims a schema_version newer than this core understands —
refusing loudly beats misreading user data (§2.2).

A `links` row must set exactly one target arm. Slice 1's arms are
`{target_object_id, unresolved_text}`; `given` is how many were set (§6.1a/§6.1b).

A deliberate edge (`from_content = false`) carries no object target — the typed
knowledge graph stays object-only, no off-graph deliberate edges (§6.1a).

`target_unit_id` is set without `target_object_id` — a unit refinement requires
its owning object target (§6.1a composite-FK discipline).

A typed graph edge resolved to a non-object target (notation/source/unresolved) —
every graph edge type requires an object target (§6.1a/§6.1b).

link_type.description:
Typed relationship of a `links` edge (§6.1b). Most are deliberate graph edges
(object-target, §6.1a); `related`/content-derived rows may stay unresolved.

oneOf.description:
A copied/derived object points back at its origin (§6.1b). Produced by the
`materialize_object` copy-and-edge stand-in (slice 1c); general enough to cover
future non-copy derivations (re-home vs origin-reference is slice 2, §18.5).

`target_selector` is set without `target_object_id` — a selector refines an object
target, never substitutes for one (§6.1a).

The derived `content_kind` would disagree with the unit's `content` tag (one fact,
one home — the generated column must equal the union tag, §6.0b).
A unit's `slot` is not valid for its parent's content kind (§6.0b/§6.1a).
`example_kind` is set on a unit whose `type` is not `example` (§6.0b).

A `*_detail.object_id` references an object of the wrong type (§6.1a
type-qualified references).

oneOf.description:
A valid, producible type that may not be minted by the DIRECT typed-create path — it
enters the graph by declaration → materialization (§9.y/§13a slice 2), never a raw POST.
Distinct from `TypeNotProducibleYet` (reserved, no machinery yet): here the machinery
exists (materialize / number / export); only the direct-create surface is gated.

Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,

oneOf.description:
A `taggings` row must target exactly one of {object, unit}; `given` is how many
were set (§6.0b).
An alias's `scope` and `scope_ref` are inconsistent (§6.1a scope ↔ scope_ref).

scope.description: The scope an alias is valid in (§6.3b); paired with `scope_ref` (§6.1a consistency).

oneOf.description:
A `handles` row must set exactly one refinement of {unit, expression}; `given` is
how many were set (§6.3b/§6.1a).

`declared_by = ai` is structurally forbidden: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` (§3.9/§6.0).

An op named a resolution/target kind whose machinery has not landed yet — e.g.
resolving an occurrence to `notation` before the notation registry exists (slice 2).
An op referenced a unit id that is not in the supplied content (stale read / bad id).
An op referenced an expression id that is not in the target unit's content.
An occurrence index is past the end of the expression's occurrence list.

`split_unit` was asked to split a unit whose content kind has no split semantics in
slice 1 (only `prose` is splittable).

`merge_units` was asked to merge units that are not mergeable (non-adjacent, different
parent/object, or a non-prose content kind).

A list of fresh ids did not match the count it had to cover (e.g. `new_tagging_ids`
vs the taggings to propagate). A glue minting bug, not a client error.

`materialize_object` was given an id remap that did not cover every {kind} in the
source content — copying with a partial map would alias ids across objects. Glue bug.
`split_unit` was asked to split past the end of the unit's prose text.

An inline atom (`math`/`reference`) carries a non-zero-width span — the prose-atom
contract requires `span.start == span.end` (its content lives in its own field, §6.0).
A glue/editor bug, not a client error.

An inline element's span falls outside its prose text — `start <= end <= text length`
(char offsets) is the §6.0 well-formedness rule. The ops maintain it by construction; the
import load path can't assume it, so it gates here (an out-of-bounds span mis-slices at the
render/editor boundary). A glue/import bug, not a client error.

An occurrence's selector span falls outside its expression's `surface_text` —
`start <= end <= surface_text length` (char offsets, §6.3a). Like inline spans, the ops
maintain it by construction (the surface serializer produces in-bounds selectors); the
import load path can't assume it, so it gates here — a corrupt selector is the slice-2
resolution substrate, so it must not reach storage. A glue/import bug, not a client error.

A content-derived edge (`from_content = true`) must record WHERE it came from —
both `source_unit_id` and `content_locator` (§6.1b).

`resolve_occurrence` was asked to resolve an occurrence that is already resolved —
re-resolving would overwrite the target and double-emit the edge (§6.3a).

`materialize_object`'s source content contains a duplicate {kind} id — copying would
alias the duplicates onto one fresh id via the remap. A glue/data bug.

CoreError.title: CoreError
CoreError.description:
Errors crossing the FFI result envelope: a domain validation failure, or input that
did not even parse as the expected shape. Tagged so the glue dispatches without
string-matching.

CreateContext.title: CreateContext
CreateContext.description:
Server-side context for a create: who/what is creating (origin + actor) and the
glue-minted provenance id. Never client-supplied.

origin.description:
Provenance origin (arch doc §6.1). Stable, complete set; only `User` and `System`
are producible until the AI/import provenance columns land with their slices.

id.description: Client-minted UUIDv7, as a string — the core parses and version-checks it.

DeclaredBy.title: DeclaredBy
DeclaredBy.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

DefinitionDetail.title: DefinitionDetail
DefinitionDetail.description:
Non-content metadata for a `definition` object (§6.1c): the definiendum, which is
object identity. The body is flowing units, not a column.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

DisplayLabels.title: DisplayLabels
DisplayLabels.description: The projection's output: one `UnitLabel` per input unit, in stable document order.

items.description:
One unit's computed label facets (§6.3b, decision G). BOTH are returned; presentation
decides whether the name supersedes the number. `number`/`name` are independently optional.

unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

unit_type.description:
Echoed so presentation can render "Theorem 3" without re-reading the unit. `None` for
a plain (typeless) unit.

number.description:
The 1-based ordinal among its counter class; `None` when the policy doesn't number this
unit's type.

name.description:
A user name override (a `handle` on the unit, or — for an object `embed` — the embedded
object's `alias`). Where present it is what a chip shows; the `number` is still returned.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

EmbedTarget.title: EmbedTarget
EmbedTarget.description:
What an `embed` unit transcludes (§6.0/§6.5). Slice 1 supports the OBJECT arm;
`source_excerpt` is reserved until sources land (slice 3).

ExampleKind.title: ExampleKind

Inline.title: Inline
Inline.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

source_object.description:

Content (`content_units`/MathContent) is a SEPARATE aggregate arriving in slice 1;

structural. Named `CanonicalObject` (not `Object`) so the generated TS type never
shadows JavaScript's global `Object`.

type.description:

proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

title.description: Tri-state (§6.3): `None` = unset, `Some("")` = explicitly empty — never collapsed.

raw_source.description: The rough input, preserved VERBATIM (§2.2). No normalization function exists.

status.description:

revision.description: Optimistic-concurrency token (§6.4): increments on every persisted write.

presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;
this is the wire shape it produces.

id.description:
Stable identity, minted in content (UUIDv7), unique workspace-wide — the
copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

surface_text.description: The canonical surface in our grammar (names-canonical `mathmeander`).

original_input.description: Raw keystrokes, preserved verbatim — NEVER overwritten by normalization (§2.2).

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,
not LaTeX. Un-parseable input is never punished: it persists as `invalid` with
`original_input` intact (§2.2).

- `renderable`    — parses cleanly into a fully-formed AST.
- `partially_resolved` — parses, but with one or more recovered error fragments
  (`Expr::Error`); still renderable, with the bad parts shown verbatim.
- `invalid`       — nothing usable parsed (e.g. empty after trimming, or all error).
- `unresolved`    — reserved for "parsed, but has unresolved occurrences/references"
  (set by the core once occurrence resolution runs; the parser never emits it).

occurrences.description:
Semantic refs inside the surface; each extracts to an edge (§6.1b). Always present
(`[]` when none) so the shape is explicit on both sides of the FFI.

items.description:
A semantic reference INSIDE a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). MVP selector is a coarse char
span (Q3); sub-term `Symbol` resolution (with `BoundVar`) is reserved. `target` is
absent while unresolved (the queue carries the unresolved text on the `links` row).

selector.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

Steps are child rows; each step child carries its relation (slice 1b+).
Each case is a child group; `slot` marks the assumption child vs body children.

Quotes & object embeds.

target.description:
What an `embed` unit transcludes (§6.0/§6.5). Slice 1 supports the OBJECT arm;
`source_excerpt` is reserved until sources land (slice 3).

items.description: One old→new unit-id mapping (materialize re-homes copied units).

to.description:

MathContent.title: MathContent
MathContent.description:
The working content aggregate an op transforms: one object's units plus its concurrency
token. NOT a persisted row — the glue assembles it from `content_units` + `objects.revision`
(§6.0b/§6.4) and writes the resulting `OpOutcome` back.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

items.description:
A content unit — the §6.0b `content_units` row. Content is the authored material;
`content_kind` is a DERIVED SQL column (the `content` tag), so it is NOT a field here.

id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

parent_unit_id.description: THE nesting mechanism: a child names its parent (must be in the same object, §6.1a).

slot.description:
Container-internal part where needed, e.g. `assumption` (case child) /
`justification` (proof_step child); `None` otherwise.

type.description: THE one user-facing math-flow label; `None` = plain content.

status.description:
Per-unit crystallization state (§6.0/§2.4) — distinct from `MathExpression.parse_status`
(surface renderability) and from `ObjectStatus` (object lifecycle).

declared_by.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

extracted_structure.description: Optional candidate decomposition — declared, never written in slice 1 (§6.0).

kind.description: A value from the small named registry (e.g. `hypothesis_conclusion_decomposition`).

accepted_into.description: The version it materialized into, once accepted (audit trail) — `None` until then.

content.description:
A SINGLE unit's own authored material (§6.0) — never an embedded unit tree (children
are rows: `parent_unit_id` + `position` + `slot`). The internal `kind` tag is the
AUTHORITATIVE discriminator and the source of the derived `content_kind` SQL column.

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

oneOf.description: A formatting region over prose `text` — `span` may be non-empty (the marked range).

identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

Mathpack.title: Mathpack
Mathpack.description:
What export produces: a deterministic manifest + the canonical graph. The glue writes this
into the actual `.mathpack` (zip + `assets/`) and stamps the checksum sidecar.

manifest.description:
The manifest header (§10). `format`/`format_version` are pinned consts; `schema_version` is
this core's `CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` at export; `created_at` is passed in (the core reads no
clock). No content-hash field lives here — the I/O shell wraps the serialized bytes with a
checksum sidecar (decision F).

counts.description: Derived element counts, for a self-describing manifest (§10) and a cheap integrity probe.

provenance.description:
The trust-spine rows travel with the graph (`MathpackGraph.provenance`), so they are
counted too — every graph vec has a count (a complete self-describing manifest).

items.description: A content-hashed asset reference (§10). The hash is glue-computed; the core only carries it.

graph.description:
The canonical graph over slice-1 entities (§10). One `MathContent` per object carries that
object's units. Deferred entity sections are ABSENT (added when their slice lands), so the
shape never claims to round-trip tables that don't exist yet.

items.description:
A canonical object — the doc's §6 `objects` row shape, verbatim (all eleven fields).
Content (`content_units`/MathContent) is a SEPARATE aggregate arriving in slice 1;
an object with zero units is valid, which is what makes that addition purely
structural. Named `CanonicalObject` (not `Object`) so the generated TS type never
shadows JavaScript's global `Object`.

id.description: Client-minted UUIDv7 (arch doc §4/§6.3).

type.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

title.description: Tri-state (§6.3): `None` = unset, `Some("")` = explicitly empty — never collapsed.

raw_source.description: The rough input, preserved VERBATIM (§2.2). No normalization function exists.

status.description:
Object lifecycle (arch doc §5.2/§6) — the full, doc-stable set lands now even though
the skeleton's create path can only PRODUCE `Draft`; other values become producible
via future status-transition operations.

schema_version.description: Application-level model version (§6.3); migrated by the core's total functions.

revision.description: Optimistic-concurrency token (§6.4): increments on every persisted write.

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

provenance.description: The provenance rows the graph references — the trust spine travels with the data.

items.description:
Provenance — the typed trust spine (arch doc §6.1). One row per provenanced fact;
origin-specific fields (model, prompt_template, context_snapshot_id, review_item_id,
source_id, source_locator) arrive as `Option` fields together with their target
tables — additive in Rust and SQL simultaneously.

id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

origin.description:
Provenance origin (arch doc §6.1). Stable, complete set; only `User` and `System`
are producible until the AI/import provenance columns land with their slices.

created_by.description:
User/agent id. Required when `origin = user` (arch doc §6.1a) — enforced by
validation, not by the shape.

items.description:
One edge of the provenance derivation chain (§6.1) — a typed, FK-checked join row
rather than a `uuid[]`, because provenance is the trust spine.

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

derived_from_provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

content.description: One per object; `MathContent.units` carries that object's content units.

shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the

identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

text.description:

expr.description:
The one structure for all math, wherever it sits (§6.3a). Identity is STABLE and
presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;

id.description:
Stable identity, minted in content (UUIDv7), unique workspace-wide — the
copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

surface_text.description: The canonical surface in our grammar (names-canonical `mathmeander`).

original_input.description: Raw keystrokes, preserved verbatim — NEVER overwritten by normalization (§2.2).

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,
not LaTeX. Un-parseable input is never punished: it persists as `invalid` with
`original_input` intact (§2.2).

- `renderable`    — parses cleanly into a fully-formed AST.
- `partially_resolved` — parses, but with one or more recovered error fragments

  (set by the core once occurrence resolution runs; the parser never emits it).

scope.description: Uniqueness/conflict scope of a handle (§6.3b).

provenance.description:
The trust-spine rows travel with the graph (`MathpackGraph.provenance`), so they are
counted too — every graph vec has a count (a complete self-describing manifest).

MathpackGraph.title: MathpackGraph
MathpackGraph.description:
The canonical graph over slice-1 entities (§10). One `MathContent` per object carries that
object's units. Deferred entity sections are ABSENT (added when their slice lands), so the
shape never claims to round-trip tables that don't exist yet.

items.description:
A canonical object — the doc's §6 `objects` row shape, verbatim (all eleven fields).
Content (`content_units`/MathContent) is a SEPARATE aggregate arriving in slice 1;
an object with zero units is valid, which is what makes that addition purely
structural. Named `CanonicalObject` (not `Object`) so the generated TS type never
shadows JavaScript's global `Object`.

id.description: Client-minted UUIDv7 (arch doc §4/§6.3).

type.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

title.description: Tri-state (§6.3): `None` = unset, `Some("")` = explicitly empty — never collapsed.

raw_source.description: The rough input, preserved VERBATIM (§2.2). No normalization function exists.

status.description:
Object lifecycle (arch doc §5.2/§6) — the full, doc-stable set lands now even though
the skeleton's create path can only PRODUCE `Draft`; other values become producible
via future status-transition operations.

revision.description: Optimistic-concurrency token (§6.4): increments on every persisted write.

source_id, source_locator) arrive as `Option` fields together with their target
tables — additive in Rust and SQL simultaneously.

id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

Provenance origin (arch doc §6.1). Stable, complete set; only `User` and `System`

User/agent id. Required when `origin = user` (arch doc §6.1a) — enforced by

One edge of the provenance derivation chain (§6.1) — a typed, FK-checked join row

content.description: One per object; `MathContent.units` carries that object's content units.

id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

parent_unit_id.description: THE nesting mechanism: a child names its parent (must be in the same object, §6.1a).

slot.description:
Container-internal part where needed, e.g. `assumption` (case child) /

type.description: THE one user-facing math-flow label; `None` = plain content.

status.description:
Per-unit crystallization state (§6.0/§2.4) — distinct from `MathExpression.parse_status`

declared_by.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

extracted_structure.description: Optional candidate decomposition — declared, never written in slice 1 (§6.0).

kind.description: A value from the small named registry (e.g. `hypothesis_conclusion_decomposition`).

accepted_into.description: The version it materialized into, once accepted (audit trail) — `None` until then.

content.description:
A SINGLE unit's own authored material (§6.0) — never an embedded unit tree (children
are rows: `parent_unit_id` + `position` + `slot`). The internal `kind` tag is the

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

MathpackImport.title: MathpackImport
MathpackImport.description:
What import yields: the validated, per-object-migrated graph with a manifest rebuilt at the
current schema version. A distinct type from `Mathpack` so import can diverge later (e.g.
id remapping on collision) without reshaping export.

manifest.description:
The manifest header (§10). `format`/`format_version` are pinned consts; `schema_version` is
this core's `CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` at export; `created_at` is passed in (the core reads no
clock). No content-hash field lives here — the I/O shell wraps the serialized bytes with a
checksum sidecar (decision F).

counts.description: Derived element counts, for a self-describing manifest (§10) and a cheap integrity probe.

provenance.description:
The trust-spine rows travel with the graph (`MathpackGraph.provenance`), so they are
counted too — every graph vec has a count (a complete self-describing manifest).

items.description: A content-hashed asset reference (§10). The hash is glue-computed; the core only carries it.

graph.description:
The canonical graph over slice-1 entities (§10). One `MathContent` per object carries that
object's units. Deferred entity sections are ABSENT (added when their slice lands), so the
shape never claims to round-trip tables that don't exist yet.

items.description:
A canonical object — the doc's §6 `objects` row shape, verbatim (all eleven fields).
Content (`content_units`/MathContent) is a SEPARATE aggregate arriving in slice 1;
an object with zero units is valid, which is what makes that addition purely
structural. Named `CanonicalObject` (not `Object`) so the generated TS type never
shadows JavaScript's global `Object`.

proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,

- `unresolved`    — reserved for "parsed, but has unresolved occurrences/references"

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

content.description:
A SINGLE unit's own authored material (§6.0) — never an embedded unit tree (children

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

oneOf.description: A formatting region over prose `text` — `span` may be non-empty (the marked range).

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

oneOf.description: Inline math — a zero-width atom (`span = [p, p]`); content lives in `expr`.

span.description:

identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`

expr.description:
The one structure for all math, wherever it sits (§6.3a). Identity is STABLE and
presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;
this is the wire shape it produces.

id.description:
Stable identity, minted in content (UUIDv7), unique workspace-wide — the
copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

surface_text.description: The canonical surface in our grammar (names-canonical `mathmeander`).

original_input.description: Raw keystrokes, preserved verbatim — NEVER overwritten by normalization (§2.2).

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,
not LaTeX. Un-parseable input is never punished: it persists as `invalid` with
`original_input` intact (§2.2).

- `renderable`    — parses cleanly into a fully-formed AST.
- `partially_resolved` — parses, but with one or more recovered error fragments
  (`Expr::Error`); still renderable, with the bad parts shown verbatim.
- `invalid`       — nothing usable parsed (e.g. empty after trimming, or all error).
- `unresolved`    — reserved for "parsed, but has unresolved occurrences/references"
  (set by the core once occurrence resolution runs; the parser never emits it).

occurrences.description:
Semantic refs inside the surface; each extracts to an edge (§6.1b). Always present
(`[]` when none) so the shape is explicit on both sides of the FFI.

items.description:
A semantic reference INSIDE a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). MVP selector is a coarse char
span (Q3); sub-term `Symbol` resolution (with `BoundVar`) is reserved. `target` is
absent while unresolved (the queue carries the unresolved text on the `links` row).

oneOf.description: Items are child rows.
Steps are child rows; each step child carries its relation (slice 1b+).
Each case is a child group; `slot` marks the assumption child vs body children.

Quotes & object embeds.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

An edge — the §6.1b `links` row. Slice 1 supports a TWO-ARM target (`target_object_id`

anyOf.description:
A finer-than-unit refinement of an edge target (§6.1d). Unit-level refinement is the

expression_id.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).

A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in

type.description:
Typed relationship of a `links` edge (§6.1b). Most are deliberate graph edges
(object-target, §6.1a); `related`/content-derived rows may stay unresolved.

oneOf.description:
A copied/derived object points back at its origin (§6.1b). Produced by the
`materialize_object` copy-and-edge stand-in (slice 1c); general enough to cover
future non-copy derivations (re-home vs origin-reference is slice 2, §18.5).

status.description:
Edge lifecycle (§6.1b). Marker-derived edges never go stale (they sync with or die
with their markers); canonical edges whose basis vanished go `stale` to review.

source_unit_id.description: Which unit the reference/occurrence sits in (composite FK to the source object).

content_locator.description: Where in that unit (§6.1d). The SQL `in_expression` column is derived from this.

anyOf.description:
Where in a unit's content an edge sits (§6.1d). Paired with the `source_unit_id`
column (the unit is context-supplied). The SQL `in_expression` column is DERIVED from
`kind = expression_span` — never written independently.

oneOf.description: A span within the unit's prose text.
A span within an expression's surface — stable across inline/display toggles (§6.3a).

expression_id.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

items.description:
An alias — a name for an EXISTING object (§6.3b). Unresolved *references* are `links`
rows carrying `unresolved_text`, not aliases (one mechanism, not two).

id.description: Identity of an alias (`aliases` row, §6.3b) — a name for an object.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

kind.description: How an alias came to name an object (§6.3b).

scope.description: The scope an alias is valid in (§6.3b); paired with `scope_ref` (§6.1a consistency).

scope_ref.description:
The scoping entity (source, trail, note…), polymorphic — core-validated against

items.description:
A handle — an optional human name for an INTRA-object element (a unit or an
expression), §6.3b. Bound to the owning object (always) refined by exactly one of
unit / expression. Objects are named by `aliases`, not handles.

id.description:
Identity of a handle (`handles` row, §6.3b) — a user name for an intra-object
element (a unit or expression).

target_object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

target_expression_id.description:
A `MathExpression` id, resolved via the expression→unit index (§6.3a). Exactly one
of `{target_unit_id, target_expression_id}` is set (§6.1a).

status.description:
Handle lifecycle (§6.3b): a handle whose target is gone goes `stale` to review,
never silently (§6.1b).

scope.description: Uniqueness/conflict scope of a handle (§6.3b).

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

items.description:
A user tag — free-form personal organization, a facet apart from `type` and edges,
with no mathematical semantics (§6.0b).

id.description: Identity of a tag (`tags` row, §6.0b).

items.description: A tagging — a tag applied to exactly one target (object xor unit), §6.0b.

id.description: Identity of a tagging (`taggings` row, §6.0b) — a tag applied to a target.

tagged_object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

tagged_unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

items.description:
A lightweight, append-only history checkpoint (§6.4). The `snapshot` is a serialized
canonical object (a snapshot/log, the §6 JSONB exception), carried opaquely by the core.

id.description: Identity of a version checkpoint (`object_versions` row, §6.4).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

items.description:
Non-content metadata for a `definition` object (§6.1c): the definiendum, which is
object identity. The body is flowing units, not a column.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

error.description:
Errors crossing the FFI result envelope: a domain validation failure, or input that
did not even parse as the expected shape. Tagged so the glue dispatches without
string-matching.

oneOf.description:
A domain validation failure. Errors are VALUES crossing the FFI (result envelopes),
never exceptions (arch doc §17 boundary discipline).
Client-minted ids must be UUIDv7 (arch doc §4/§6.3 — sortable, client-mintable).
First §6.1a origin-field invariant: `created_by` is required when origin = user.

origin.description:
Provenance origin (arch doc §6.1). Stable, complete set; only `User` and `System`
are producible until the AI/import provenance columns land with their slices.

oneOf.description: AI/import provenance is structurally impossible until its columns land (§6.1).

origin.description:
Provenance origin (arch doc §6.1). Stable, complete set; only `User` and `System`
are producible until the AI/import provenance columns land with their slices.

oneOf.description:
A stored value claims a schema_version newer than this core understands —
refusing loudly beats misreading user data (§2.2).

A `links` row must set exactly one target arm. Slice 1's arms are
`{target_object_id, unresolved_text}`; `given` is how many were set (§6.1a/§6.1b).

A deliberate edge (`from_content = false`) carries no object target — the typed
knowledge graph stays object-only, no off-graph deliberate edges (§6.1a).

`target_unit_id` is set without `target_object_id` — a unit refinement requires
its owning object target (§6.1a composite-FK discipline).

A typed graph edge resolved to a non-object target (notation/source/unresolved) —
every graph edge type requires an object target (§6.1a/§6.1b).

link_type.description:
Typed relationship of a `links` edge (§6.1b). Most are deliberate graph edges
(object-target, §6.1a); `related`/content-derived rows may stay unresolved.

oneOf.description:
A copied/derived object points back at its origin (§6.1b). Produced by the
`materialize_object` copy-and-edge stand-in (slice 1c); general enough to cover
future non-copy derivations (re-home vs origin-reference is slice 2, §18.5).

`target_selector` is set without `target_object_id` — a selector refines an object
target, never substitutes for one (§6.1a).

The derived `content_kind` would disagree with the unit's `content` tag (one fact,
one home — the generated column must equal the union tag, §6.0b).
A unit's `slot` is not valid for its parent's content kind (§6.0b/§6.1a).
`example_kind` is set on a unit whose `type` is not `example` (§6.0b).

A `*_detail.object_id` references an object of the wrong type (§6.1a
type-qualified references).

expected.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

given.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

oneOf.description:
The create path cannot produce this object type yet (reserved vocabulary whose
owning machinery lands in a later slice, §6.1a/§13a).

object_type.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

oneOf.description:
A valid, producible type that may not be minted by the DIRECT typed-create path — it
enters the graph by declaration → materialization (§9.y/§13a slice 2), never a raw POST.
Distinct from `TypeNotProducibleYet` (reserved, no machinery yet): here the machinery
exists (materialize / number / export); only the direct-create surface is gated.

object_type.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

oneOf.description:
A `taggings` row must target exactly one of {object, unit}; `given` is how many
were set (§6.0b).
An alias's `scope` and `scope_ref` are inconsistent (§6.1a scope ↔ scope_ref).

scope.description: The scope an alias is valid in (§6.3b); paired with `scope_ref` (§6.1a consistency).

oneOf.description:
A `handles` row must set exactly one refinement of {unit, expression}; `given` is
how many were set (§6.3b/§6.1a).

`declared_by = ai` is structurally forbidden: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` (§3.9/§6.0).

An op named a resolution/target kind whose machinery has not landed yet — e.g.
resolving an occurrence to `notation` before the notation registry exists (slice 2).
An op referenced a unit id that is not in the supplied content (stale read / bad id).
An op referenced an expression id that is not in the target unit's content.
An occurrence index is past the end of the expression's occurrence list.

`split_unit` was asked to split a unit whose content kind has no split semantics in
slice 1 (only `prose` is splittable).

`merge_units` was asked to merge units that are not mergeable (non-adjacent, different
parent/object, or a non-prose content kind).

A list of fresh ids did not match the count it had to cover (e.g. `new_tagging_ids`
vs the taggings to propagate). A glue minting bug, not a client error.

`materialize_object` was given an id remap that did not cover every {kind} in the
source content — copying with a partial map would alias ids across objects. Glue bug.
`split_unit` was asked to split past the end of the unit's prose text.

An inline atom (`math`/`reference`) carries a non-zero-width span — the prose-atom
contract requires `span.start == span.end` (its content lives in its own field, §6.0).
A glue/editor bug, not a client error.

An inline element's span falls outside its prose text — `start <= end <= text length`
(char offsets) is the §6.0 well-formedness rule. The ops maintain it by construction; the
import load path can't assume it, so it gates here (an out-of-bounds span mis-slices at the
render/editor boundary). A glue/import bug, not a client error.

An occurrence's selector span falls outside its expression's `surface_text` —
`start <= end <= surface_text length` (char offsets, §6.3a). Like inline spans, the ops
maintain it by construction (the surface serializer produces in-bounds selectors); the
import load path can't assume it, so it gates here — a corrupt selector is the slice-2
resolution substrate, so it must not reach storage. A glue/import bug, not a client error.

A content-derived edge (`from_content = true`) must record WHERE it came from —
both `source_unit_id` and `content_locator` (§6.1b).

`resolve_occurrence` was asked to resolve an occurrence that is already resolved —
re-resolving would overwrite the target and double-emit the edge (§6.3a).

`materialize_object`'s source content contains a duplicate {kind} id — copying would
alias the duplicates onto one fresh id via the remap. A glue/data bug.

MathpackManifest.title: MathpackManifest
MathpackManifest.description:
The manifest header (§10). `format`/`format_version` are pinned consts; `schema_version` is
this core's `CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` at export; `created_at` is passed in (the core reads no
clock). No content-hash field lives here — the I/O shell wraps the serialized bytes with a
checksum sidecar (decision F).

counts.description: Derived element counts, for a self-describing manifest (§10) and a cheap integrity probe.

provenance.description:
The trust-spine rows travel with the graph (`MathpackGraph.provenance`), so they are
counted too — every graph vec has a count (a complete self-describing manifest).

items.description: A content-hashed asset reference (§10). The hash is glue-computed; the core only carries it.

MathpackMeta.title: MathpackMeta
MathpackMeta.description:
The glue-supplied scalars the core cannot derive itself (the space, and asset checksums
computed in the I/O shell). Counts and the format/version/schema fields are core-derived.

asset_checksums.description:
One entry per referenced asset; `sha256` is computed in the I/O shell and handed in
(the core never hashes). Empty in slice 1 (no assets until sources, slice 3).

items.description: A content-hashed asset reference (§10). The hash is glue-computed; the core only carries it.

value.description:
What export produces: a deterministic manifest + the canonical graph. The glue writes this
into the actual `.mathpack` (zip + `assets/`) and stamps the checksum sidecar.

manifest.description:
The manifest header (§10). `format`/`format_version` are pinned consts; `schema_version` is
this core's `CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` at export; `created_at` is passed in (the core reads no
clock). No content-hash field lives here — the I/O shell wraps the serialized bytes with a
checksum sidecar (decision F).

counts.description: Derived element counts, for a self-describing manifest (§10) and a cheap integrity probe.

provenance.description:
The trust-spine rows travel with the graph (`MathpackGraph.provenance`), so they are
counted too — every graph vec has a count (a complete self-describing manifest).

items.description: A content-hashed asset reference (§10). The hash is glue-computed; the core only carries it.

graph.description:
The canonical graph over slice-1 entities (§10). One `MathContent` per object carries that
object's units. Deferred entity sections are ABSENT (added when their slice lands), so the
shape never claims to round-trip tables that don't exist yet.

items.description:
A canonical object — the doc's §6 `objects` row shape, verbatim (all eleven fields).
Content (`content_units`/MathContent) is a SEPARATE aggregate arriving in slice 1;
an object with zero units is valid, which is what makes that addition purely

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,
not LaTeX. Un-parseable input is never punished: it persists as `invalid` with

- `renderable`    — parses cleanly into a fully-formed AST.
- `partially_resolved` — parses, but with one or more recovered error fragments

- `invalid`       — nothing usable parsed (e.g. empty after trimming, or all error).
- `unresolved`    — reserved for "parsed, but has unresolved occurrences/references"
  (set by the core once occurrence resolution runs; the parser never emits it).

type-qualified references).

oneOf.description:
A `handles` row must set exactly one refinement of {unit, expression}; `given` is
how many were set (§6.3b/§6.1a).

`declared_by = ai` is structurally forbidden: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` (§3.9/§6.0).

An op named a resolution/target kind whose machinery has not landed yet — e.g.
resolving an occurrence to `notation` before the notation registry exists (slice 2).
An op referenced a unit id that is not in the supplied content (stale read / bad id).
An op referenced an expression id that is not in the target unit's content.
An occurrence index is past the end of the expression's occurrence list.

`split_unit` was asked to split a unit whose content kind has no split semantics in
slice 1 (only `prose` is splittable).

`merge_units` was asked to merge units that are not mergeable (non-adjacent, different
parent/object, or a non-prose content kind).

A list of fresh ids did not match the count it had to cover (e.g. `new_tagging_ids`
vs the taggings to propagate). A glue minting bug, not a client error.

`materialize_object` was given an id remap that did not cover every {kind} in the
source content — copying with a partial map would alias ids across objects. Glue bug.
`split_unit` was asked to split past the end of the unit's prose text.

An inline atom (`math`/`reference`) carries a non-zero-width span — the prose-atom
contract requires `span.start == span.end` (its content lives in its own field, §6.0).
A glue/editor bug, not a client error.

An inline element's span falls outside its prose text — `start <= end <= text length`
(char offsets) is the §6.0 well-formedness rule. The ops maintain it by construction; the
import load path can't assume it, so it gates here (an out-of-bounds span mis-slices at the
render/editor boundary). A glue/import bug, not a client error.

An occurrence's selector span falls outside its expression's `surface_text` —
`start <= end <= surface_text length` (char offsets, §6.3a). Like inline spans, the ops
maintain it by construction (the surface serializer produces in-bounds selectors); the
import load path can't assume it, so it gates here — a corrupt selector is the slice-2
resolution substrate, so it must not reach storage. A glue/import bug, not a client error.

A content-derived edge (`from_content = true`) must record WHERE it came from —
both `source_unit_id` and `content_locator` (§6.1b).

`resolve_occurrence` was asked to resolve an occurrence that is already resolved —
re-resolving would overwrite the target and double-emit the edge (§6.3a).

NumberingPolicy.title: NumberingPolicy
NumberingPolicy.description:
Presentation config for the numbering projection (§6.3b) — passed in, never stored, so
the core stays policy-free. The numbering *policy* (which types count, one counter vs
per-type) lives entirely here.

numbered_types.description:
The unit types that participate in numbering. A unit whose `type` is absent from this
list gets no number (`number = None`). Order is irrelevant to the result.

items.description:
THE one user-facing math-flow label of a unit (§6.0). Carries *force and meaning*;
form lives separately in `UnitContent.kind`. Absent (`Option::None`) = plain content.
Forms (list/group/embed/derivation/case_split) are deliberately NOT here — they are
content kinds, never types (§6.0b).

shared_counter.description:
`true` → ONE counter runs across every numbered type (a single document sequence:
Theorem 1, Definition 2, Lemma 3…); `false` → each type counts independently
(Theorem 1, Theorem 2, Definition 1…).

value.description: The projection's output: one `UnitLabel` per input unit, in stable document order.

items.description:
One unit's computed label facets (§6.3b, decision G). BOTH are returned; presentation
decides whether the name supersedes the number. `number`/`name` are independently optional.

unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

unit_type.description:
Echoed so presentation can render "Theorem 3" without re-reading the unit. `None` for
a plain (typeless) unit.

number.description:
The 1-based ordinal among its counter class; `None` when the policy doesn't number this
unit's type.

name.description:
A user name override (a `handle` on the unit, or — for an object `embed` — the embedded
object's `alias`). Where present it is what a chip shows; the `number` is still returned.

string-matching.

alias the duplicates onto one fresh id via the remap. A glue/data bug.

OpContext.description:
Glue-supplied minting context for an op (mirrors `validate::CreateContext`). `now` is a
separate fn arg, like `create_object`. Ids are typed newtypes (glue-minted/trusted — a
malformed one is a glue bug surfaced as `MalformedInput` at the FFI boundary, slice 1d).

version_id.description: Pre-minted id for this op's `version_snapshot` row.

OpOutcome.title: OpOutcome
OpOutcome.description:
Everything an op produces, for the glue to persist in one transaction. Vectors are empty
for ops that don't touch that facet (e.g. only `materialize_object` fills `new_objects`).

content.description:
The working content aggregate an op transforms: one object's units plus its concurrency
token. NOT a persisted row — the glue assembles it from `content_units` + `objects.revision`
(§6.0b/§6.4) and writes the resulting `OpOutcome` back.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

items.description:
A content unit — the §6.0b `content_units` row. Content is the authored material;
`content_kind` is a DERIVED SQL column (the `content` tag), so it is NOT a field here.

id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

parent_unit_id.description: THE nesting mechanism: a child names its parent (must be in the same object, §6.1a).

slot.description:
Container-internal part where needed, e.g. `assumption` (case child) /
`justification` (proof_step child); `None` otherwise.

type.description: THE one user-facing math-flow label; `None` = plain content.

status.description:
Per-unit crystallization state (§6.0/§2.4) — distinct from `MathExpression.parse_status`
(surface renderability) and from `ObjectStatus` (object lifecycle).

declared_by.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

extracted_structure.description: Optional candidate decomposition — declared, never written in slice 1 (§6.0).

kind.description: A value from the small named registry (e.g. `hypothesis_conclusion_decomposition`).

accepted_into.description: The version it materialized into, once accepted (audit trail) — `None` until then.

content.description:
A SINGLE unit's own authored material (§6.0) — never an embedded unit tree (children
are rows: `parent_unit_id` + `position` + `slot`). The internal `kind` tag is the
AUTHORITATIVE discriminator and the source of the derived `content_kind` SQL column.

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

oneOf.description: A formatting region over prose `text` — `span` may be non-empty (the marked range).

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

oneOf.description: Inline math — a zero-width atom (`span = [p, p]`); content lives in `expr`.

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

expr.description:
The one structure for all math, wherever it sits (§6.3a). Identity is STABLE and
presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;
this is the wire shape it produces.

id.description:
Stable identity, minted in content (UUIDv7), unique workspace-wide — the
copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

surface_text.description: The canonical surface in our grammar (names-canonical `mathmeander`).

original_input.description: Raw keystrokes, preserved verbatim — NEVER overwritten by normalization (§2.2).

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,
not LaTeX. Un-parseable input is never punished: it persists as `invalid` with
`original_input` intact (§2.2).

- `renderable`    — parses cleanly into a fully-formed AST.
- `partially_resolved` — parses, but with one or more recovered error fragments
  (`Expr::Error`); still renderable, with the bad parts shown verbatim.
- `invalid`       — nothing usable parsed (e.g. empty after trimming, or all error).
- `unresolved`    — reserved for "parsed, but has unresolved occurrences/references"
  (set by the core once occurrence resolution runs; the parser never emits it).

occurrences.description:
Semantic refs inside the surface; each extracts to an edge (§6.1b). Always present
(`[]` when none) so the shape is explicit on both sides of the FFI.

items.description:
A semantic reference INSIDE a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). MVP selector is a coarse char
span (Q3); sub-term `Symbol` resolution (with `BoundVar`) is reserved. `target` is
absent while unresolved (the queue carries the unresolved text on the `links` row).

What an `embed` unit transcludes (§6.0/§6.5). Slice 1 supports the OBJECT arm;

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

links_upserted.description:
Full edge rows to insert/update (`insert_reference`, `resolve_occurrence`, the
re-anchored edges from `rewrite_surface`, the materialize edge).

items.description:
An edge — the §6.1b `links` row. Slice 1 supports a TWO-ARM target (`target_object_id`
xor `unresolved_text`); the notation/source arms widen in slices 2/3 when their FK
targets exist. `target_unit_id`/`target_selector` are REFINEMENTS of the object target,
never target alternatives. `in_expression` is a DERIVED SQL column (not a field).

id.description: Identity of an edge (`links` row, §6.1b).

target_object_id.description: The object target (one of the two slice-1 target arms).

unresolved_text.description: The literal reference text while UNRESOLVED — the other slice-1 target arm.

target_selector.description: Finer-than-unit refinement of the object target (§6.1d).

anyOf.description:
A finer-than-unit refinement of an edge target (§6.1d). Unit-level refinement is the
FK column `target_unit_id` (never duplicated as a selector — one home). `StructuralPath`
(sub-term addressing) is reserved (§14 structural editing) and deliberately NOT declared
in slice 1.

expression_id.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the

type.description:
Typed relationship of a `links` edge (§6.1b). Most are deliberate graph edges
(object-target, §6.1a); `related`/content-derived rows may stay unresolved.

oneOf.description:
A copied/derived object points back at its origin (§6.1b). Produced by the
`materialize_object` copy-and-edge stand-in (slice 1c); general enough to cover
future non-copy derivations (re-home vs origin-reference is slice 2, §18.5).

status.description:
Edge lifecycle (§6.1b). Marker-derived edges never go stale (they sync with or die
with their markers); canonical edges whose basis vanished go `stale` to review.

source_unit_id.description: Which unit the reference/occurrence sits in (composite FK to the source object).

content_locator.description: Where in that unit (§6.1d). The SQL `in_expression` column is derived from this.

anyOf.description:
Where in a unit's content an edge sits (§6.1d). Paired with the `source_unit_id`
column (the unit is context-supplied). The SQL `in_expression` column is DERIVED from
`kind = expression_span` — never written independently.

oneOf.description: A span within the unit's prose text.
A span within an expression's surface — stable across inline/display toggles (§6.3a).

expression_id.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

links_staled.description:
Edges whose anchor could not be re-placed → the glue marks them stale, never drops
(§6.1b). A staled edge is reported here ONLY (an id), not also in `links_upserted`.

items.description: Identity of an edge (`links` row, §6.1b).

expression_id_remap.description: Old→new expression ids; EMPTY unless ids were re-minted (materialize only).

items.description: One old→new expression-id mapping (materialize copies mint fresh ids, §6.3a).

from.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

to.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-

Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

version_snapshot.description: The append-only history checkpoint for this write (§6.4).

id.description: Identity of a version checkpoint (`object_versions` row, §6.4).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

new_objects.description: Newly created objects (materialize's copy); empty otherwise.

items.description:
A canonical object — the doc's §6 `objects` row shape, verbatim (all eleven fields).
Content (`content_units`/MathContent) is a SEPARATE aggregate arriving in slice 1;
an object with zero units is valid, which is what makes that addition purely
structural. Named `CanonicalObject` (not `Object`) so the generated TS type never
shadows JavaScript's global `Object`.

id.description: Client-minted UUIDv7 (arch doc §4/§6.3).

type.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus source_excerpt, trail,
annotation, journal_day, which are **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet
producible — their detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

title.description: Tri-state (§6.3): `None` = unset, `Some("")` = explicitly empty — never collapsed.

raw_source.description: The rough input, preserved VERBATIM (§2.2). No normalization function exists.

status.description:
Object lifecycle (arch doc §5.2/§6) — the full, doc-stable set lands now even though
the skeleton's create path can only PRODUCE `Draft`; other values become producible
via future status-transition operations.

schema_version.description: Application-level model version (§6.3); migrated by the core's total functions.

revision.description: Optimistic-concurrency token (§6.4): increments on every persisted write.

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

items.description: A tagging — a tag applied to exactly one target (object xor unit), §6.0b.

id.description: Identity of a tagging (`taggings` row, §6.0b) — a tag applied to a target.

tagged_object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

tagged_unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

value.description:
Everything an op produces, for the glue to persist in one transaction. Vectors are empty
for ops that don't touch that facet (e.g. only `materialize_object` fills `new_objects`).

content.description:
The working content aggregate an op transforms: one object's units plus its concurrency
token. NOT a persisted row — the glue assembles it from `content_units` + `objects.revision`
(§6.0b/§6.4) and writes the resulting `OpOutcome` back.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

items.description:
A content unit — the §6.0b `content_units` row. Content is the authored material;
`content_kind` is a DERIVED SQL column (the `content` tag), so it is NOT a field here.

id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

parent_unit_id.description: THE nesting mechanism: a child names its parent (must be in the same object, §6.1a).

slot.description:
Container-internal part where needed, e.g. `assumption` (case child) /
`justification` (proof_step child); `None` otherwise.

type.description: THE one user-facing math-flow label; `None` = plain content.

status.description:
Per-unit crystallization state (§6.0/§2.4) — distinct from `MathExpression.parse_status`
(surface renderability) and from `ObjectStatus` (object lifecycle).

declared_by.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

extracted_structure.description: Optional candidate decomposition — declared, never written in slice 1 (§6.0).

kind.description: A value from the small named registry (e.g. `hypothesis_conclusion_decomposition`).

accepted_into.description: The version it materialized into, once accepted (audit trail) — `None` until then.

content.description:
A SINGLE unit's own authored material (§6.0) — never an embedded unit tree (children
are rows: `parent_unit_id` + `position` + `slot`). The internal `kind` tag is the
AUTHORITATIVE discriminator and the source of the derived `content_kind` SQL column.

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

oneOf.description: A formatting region over prose `text` — `span` may be non-empty (the marked range).

A `links` row must set exactly one target arm. Slice 1's arms are

`target_unit_id` is set without `target_object_id` — a unit refinement requires
